description: Understanding computational mesh structure for finite volume CFD
roadmap_reference: Day 05: Points, Faces, Cells definitions, Owner-Neighbor concept, Cylindrical mesh considerations, Face area vectors, Cell volumes

content.title: What is Mesh Topology?
content.detail: The mesh topology describes how geometric entities (points, faces, cells) are connected to form the computational domain for CFD simulations

content.title: Why Topology Matters
content.detail: Mesh topology enables flux calculation between cells, boundary condition application, and matrix assembly in finite volume method

content.title: Points (Vertices)
content.detail: Points are the fundamental geometric entities defining mesh vertices with (x, y, z) coordinates. Stored as pointField in OpenFOAM.

content.title: Faces
content.detail: A face is a list of point labels forming a polygon. Inherits from labelList. Provides area vector, centre, and normal calculations.

content.title: Cells
content.detail: A cell is a list of faces forming a polyhedron. Inherits from labelList. Provides volume and centre calculations.

content.title: Class Hierarchy: Mesh Elements
content.detail: mermaid class diagram showing face and cell inheritance from labelList

content.title: Face Area Vector
content.detail: The face area vector points in the direction of the face normal with magnitude equal to the face area

content.title: Unit Normal Vector
content.detail: The unit normal vector is the normalized face area vector

content.title: Face Centre Calculation
content.detail: The face centre is calculated as the average of its vertex point positions

content.title: Face Geometry Visual
content.detail: mermaid diagram showing face with vertices, centre, normal, and area vector

content.title: Cell Volume Calculation
content.detail: Cell volume is calculated by decomposing the cell into pyramids from the cell centre to each face

content.title: Cell Centre Calculation
content.detail: The cell centre is the area-weighted average of its face centres

content.title: Cell Geometry Visual
content.detail: mermaid diagram showing polyhedral cell with faces, centre, and volume

content.title: Owner Cell
content.detail: For each internal face, the owner cell is the one with the lower index. The face normal points FROM owner TO neighbour.

content.title: Neighbour Cell
content.detail: For each internal face, the neighbour cell is the one with the higher index. Only exists for internal faces.

content.title: Internal vs Boundary Faces
content.detail: Internal faces have both owner AND neighbour cells. Boundary faces have ONLY owner cell (neighbour = -1).

content.title: Face Orientation Convention
content.detail: The face area vector always points from the owner cell toward the neighbour cell. This is crucial for consistent flux calculations.

content.title: Owner-Neighbour Data Structure
content.detail: Show owner[] and neighbour[] arrays with example values

content.title: Owner-Neighbour Visual
content.detail: mermaid diagram showing two adjacent cells with owner, neighbour, face, and normal direction

content.title: OpenFOAM Mesh Classes
content.detail: mermaid class diagram showing primitiveMesh -> polyMesh -> fvMesh inheritance chain

content.title: primitiveMesh: Cell-Face Engine
content.detail: Base class providing mesh analysis: cell-cell, cell-face, point-face connectivity, geometric calculations

content.title: polyMesh: Polyhedral Mesh
content.detail: Adds topological data (points, faces, owner, neighbour) and boundary information

content.title: fvMesh: Finite Volume Mesh
content.detail: fvMesh inherits from polyMesh, lduMesh, and surfaceInterpolation (multiple inheritance). Adds FVM-specific data: cell volumes, face areas, cell centres, surface interpolation, schemes

content.title: Axisymmetric Simulation
content.detail: For axisymmetric problems (like flow in a tube), we can use a 2D wedge mesh to represent 3D geometry

content.title: Wedge Boundary Condition
content.detail: Wedge patches enforce cyclic symmetry conditions, reducing 3D problem to 2D wedge with small angular extent (typically 1-5 degrees)

content.title: Cell Volume in Wedge Mesh
content.detail: In wedge mesh, cell volumes include the angular factor (2π/N where N = 360°/wedge_angle) to account for full 360° geometry

content.title: Cylindrical Coordinate System
content.detail: For tube flow, use radial (r), axial (z), and circumferential (θ) coordinates. Wedge mesh lies in r-z plane.

content.title: R410A Evaporator Tube Mesh
content.detail: Application: R410A evaporator tube requires cylindrical mesh with radial resolution for boundary layer, axial resolution for flow development, and wedge for axisymmetric 2D simulation

content.title: Face Value Interpolation
content.detail: Face values are interpolated from cell-centred values using owner (P) and neighbour (N) values

content.title: surfaceInterpolationScheme Class
content.detail: Abstract base class for surface interpolation schemes (upwind, central, linear, etc.)

content.title: Interpolation Visual
content.detail: mermaid diagram showing owner cell P, neighbour cell N, face f, and interpolation weights

content.title: Gauss Theorem for Cell
content.detail: Volume integral converted to surface sum over faces using face area vectors

content.title: Flux Calculation
content.detail: Flux through each face = velocity at face dotted with face area vector. Sum over all faces gives net flux out of cell

content.title: Gauss Theorem Visual
content.detail: mermaid diagram showing cell with faces and area vectors for flux calculation

content.title: Mesh Geometry Fields
content.detail: Table showing: Sf (face area vectors), magSf (face areas), V (cell volumes), C (cell centres)

content.title: Addressing Arrays
content.detail: Table showing: owner[], neighbour[], points[], faces[]

content.title: Accessing Face Area Vector
content.detail: C++ code: const surfaceVectorField& Sf = mesh.Sf(); vector faceArea = Sf[facei];

content.title: Accessing Cell Volume
content.detail: C++ code: const volScalarField& V = mesh.V(); scalar cellVol = V[celli];

content.title: Accessing Owner-Neighbour
content.detail: C++ code: const labelUList& owner = mesh.owner(); const labelUList& neighbour = mesh.neighbour();

content.title: Exercise 1: Owner-Neighbour Quiz
content.detail: For a mesh with 5 cells, if face 10 has owner=2 and neighbour=4, what is the direction of the face normal? What if it's a boundary face?

content.title: Exercise 2: Face Area Calculation
content.detail: A triangular face has vertices at (0,0,0), (1,0,0), (0,1,0). Calculate: (a) face centre, (b) face area vector, (c) unit normal

content.title: Exercise 3: Cell Volume from Faces
content.detail: Given a hexahedral cell with 6 faces, explain how the cell volume is computed using the pyramid decomposition method

content.title: Exercise 4: Cylindrical Mesh Design
content.detail: Design a wedge mesh for R410A evaporator tube with inner radius 5mm and length 500mm. Specify: wedge angle, number of radial cells, number of axial cells

content.title: Exercise 5: Flux Through Face
content.detail: Given velocity at cell P: U_P = (1,0,0) m/s, at cell N: U_N = (2,0,0) m/s, face normal n_f = (1,0,0), face area = 0.001 m², calculate mass flux using upwind scheme (ρ=1000 kg/m³)

content.title: Exercise 6: Gauss Theorem Application
content.detail: For a cell with 4 faces, given face area vectors and velocities at each face, calculate the net volumetric flux out of the cell. Verify continuity equation.
